   Title: Not for iron sights
   Star rating: 20%
   Date posted: 4/19/23
   Full review text: First of all, writing the review, it asked me to rate warmth? WTF? Product is only good for scopes, forget iron sights. Terrible manual, website is no better. Might work good for scoops, I do not know, I returned it. Good quality from the looks of it.
   Flags:

3) Reviewer: Matthew bass player
   Title: Pretty good boresighter
   Star rating: 80%
   Date posted: 4/19/23
   Full review text: This boresighter works pretty good, if you put the scope right on center with a 22Lr or equivalent youll be almost centered, I used it on my sks and the the sights have to be just a hair above center line to hit a 6 inch circle at 100 yards, I also used it on 3 mosins, one of them with a scope, I had to put the scope 4 squares above center to hit target, the other 2 mosins have iron sights and those got set at 2 above and the other one at 3 above. Its a little tricky to use at 1st, but it works, and its fairly good for getting on paper, I used it at the range, which was nice to help get on paper 1st.... when I sighted in scoped mosin on center line it hit high at 100 yards, (like 14 inches) so I went 2 squares up from center at first, then shot, still high but better, then went 4 squares above, and made 6 shots in 6 inch circle at 100 yards, the rifle was rested with sand bags and I barely touched the gun to fire, just to pull trigger and just having my shoulder against it. I am happy with it, when using it just dont let it shift around in bore., the arbors work ,but still be careful, It will not work on any raised scope like a AR 15 or M4 so keep that in mind My thoughts is if its a high powered round go up from center 1st, if above you shoot low if under center you shoot high,, for me center line only worked on my 22 lr rifle, bring it with you to the range and get on paper. vertically I had no issues with left or right with my rifles......kinda wierd
   Flags:
   - (b) Compatibility notes: Yes — says it will not work on any raised scope like an AR-15 or M4; includes notes for iron sights and scoped rifles.

4) Reviewer: Pamela Birchenough
   Title: Claims are very misleading!
   Star rating: 20%
   Date posted: 4/19/23
   Full review text: I set my rifle up using the Bushnell Professional Bore Sighter. Using a bench vice, the first shot did not put me on paper at 50 yards. I had a 20" x 20" target and missed by a mile. Luckily I hit the backstop. 8" low from center and to the right 18". Bushnell claims it will put sighting-in test shots "on the paper". I had similar results using different guns. I Would Not recommend this product.
   Flags: None

5) Reviewer: Rick Money
   Title: Zeroing in
   Star rating: 80%
   Date posted: 4/19/23
   Full review text: Watched a retired lifetime gunsmith use one to boresight a rifle/scope project of mine -- a semi-auto which could not be boresighted the traditional way. Sure enough, he got me very close at 100 yards. I researched it, the price was reasonable, and so I bought one for myself. What truly surprised me was that my brand new one seems to be right there in quality comparison with the smiths 20+ year old unit. Nice case, decently built, the biggest difference being that -- despite what the product photo depicts above -- the cases now come with a weird magnetic latching system for the flap. Seems cheesy, but works well.....so far. My biggest squawk? It isn't very close to true zero, but at least it is consistent. Once I figured out that it would always put the first shot three inches high and two left, I could adjust the scopes reticles against the internal collimating grid to compensate and get a near-bullseye on the first shot thereafter. This will definitely get you close to zero at 100 yards on your first shot thus saving precious ammo at the range the next day. I also own a laser type boresighter and this Bushnell seems to be a better choice. The lasers are all so stinking dim that they can only be used outdoors at dusk and at very close ranges, whereas this Bushnell unit can be used indoors from the comfort of your own living room and no scrambling for oddball batteries at the last minute.
   Flags:
   - (a) Newer/better versions/alternatives: Yes — compares to a laser type boresighter and says this Bushnell is a better choice.

6) Reviewer: Whispertools
   Title: M1A bore sighting
   Star rating: 100%
   Date posted: 4/19/23
   Full review text: Initially tried to use the BoreSighter on a M1A rifle with Springfield flash hider but could not obtain consistent results using the included arbor in the flash hider. I then tried a Springfield flash suppressor alignment gauge as a mount for the Boresighter. I placed a small O ring over the shaft of the gauge up against the handle. Placed the gauge in the barrel with the handle against the flash hider, the O ring compressed in the mouth of the flash hider preventing the gauge from rotating. I was then able to easily bore sight the rifle, and duplicate the results several times. The result of the bore sighting placed the shots near the bullseye for an easy zero.
   Flags:
   - (b) Compatibility notes: Yes — notes issues using included arbor with M1A and Springfield flash hider; successful method using a Springfield flash suppressor alignment gauge with an O-ring.

7) Reviewer: LL Dude
   Title: Easy to use, and works like a charm.
   Star rating: 100%
   Date posted: 4/19/23
   Full review text: I was kinda skeptical, and didn't know what to expect when I ordered this. But, boy, am I glad I did. I had a rifle that I had just put a new variable power scope on. It really needed to be bore sighted. I verified that the scope was level to the rifle, grabbed the correct insert, and put it in the sighting scope, and placed it in the barrel. Using a lead sled to keep the rifle steady, it's just a matter of putting the scope cross hairs on the center of the little target you see in the boresight. Easy. So, took the rifle out to zero it today. WOW! I am impressed. Dead on left to right, and only about 5 inches high. A few clicks, to adjust the height, and couple more rounds to verify, and it was dead on. The cost saving on "shooting around" to find the zero amazing. Total of 8 rounds fired and gun is dead on. I would recommend this tool.
   Flags: None
